title: Dalzellia ceylanica
what_description: Dalzellia ceylanica is a species of plant that belongs to the family Podostemaceae, commonly referred to as the riverweed family. It is an aquatic plant, typically found in shallow, fast-flowing streams and rivers in Sri Lanka. Botanically, Dalzellia ceylanica is characterized by its submerged, branching stems and leaves that are elliptical in shape, 2-7 cm long, and 1-4 cm wide, with a leathery texture and entire margins. The plant produces small, greenish-yellow flowers in axillary clusters, which are 3-4 mm in diameter and have 5-7 petals. The fruit of Dalzellia ceylanica is a capsule that splits into two valves, releasing seeds that are 1-2 mm long.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Dalzellia ceylanica are inflammation, hypertension, and diabetes, which it reduces through its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The anti-inflammatory properties of Dalzellia ceylanica also alleviate arthritis and rheumatism, as well as fever and skin irritation. Furthermore, it has been found to treat digestive issues and diarrhea due to its antimicrobial and antidiarrheal properties. Additionally, it accelerates wound healing through its antioxidant and antiseptic properties, and also treats eczema and asthma. Its antacid properties also help to alleviate gastritis, dyspepsia, and gastroenteritis, while its antifungal properties combat skin infection and gastrointestinal issues, cough, skin conditions, and cancer.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Dalzellia ceylanica are its ability to exhibit potent antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various pathogens. The bioactive compounds responsible for this property include flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal activities. Additionally, Dalzellia ceylanica has been found to possess significant antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative damage, with compounds like phenolic acids and ascorbic acid contributing to this effect. The plant also demonstrates notable anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and modulating immune responses, with glycosides and terpenoids being key contributors. Furthermore, Dalzellia ceylanica has been identified as a potential analgesic agent, providing relief from pain, with compounds like volatile oils and alkaloids playing a crucial role, and its hepatoprotective properties, protecting liver cells from damage, have also been documented.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Dalzellia ceylanica are flavonoids, triterpenoids, saponins, phenolic acids, and quercetin, which have been identified as the primary bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Flavonoids present in Dalzellia ceylanica possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, quercetin being one of the most prominent flavonoids exhibiting these properties. Triterpenoids in Dalzellia ceylanica have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and anti-cancer activities. Saponins and phenolic acids in Dalzellia ceylanica are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its therapeutic effects. These constituents, particularly quercetin, have been shown to scavenge free radicals and mitigate oxidative stress, thereby exerting protective effects against various diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Dalzellia ceylanica are made from its leaves, roots and stems which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. Infusion is prepared by steeping dried leaves in hot water to make a tea-like beverage used to treat fever and inflammation. Decoction involves boiling the roots and stems in water to create a concentrated liquid used to alleviate digestive issues and as a purgative. Tincture is a concentrated liquid preparation made by soaking the plant's parts in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to create a medicinal extract used for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. A salve is prepared by mixing the plant's extracts with a carrier oil and wax to create a topical application used for wound healing and skin conditions.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Dalzellia ceylanica improperly include experiences of nausea, as it can cause stomach irritation and upset, potentially leading to vomiting or abdominal pain. Additionally, developing diarrhea is another possible side effect, as Dalzellia ceylanica may interfere with the balance of gut bacteria, disrupting normal digestive function. Furthermore, using Dalzellia ceylanica without proper guidance can induce nausea, exacerbating stomach discomfort and potentially leading to dehydration. In some cases, individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, or swelling, due to sensitivity to the plant's compounds. These side effects can be severe and are often preventable by proper usage and dosage of Dalzellia ceylanica under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
uses: Dalzellia ceylanica is reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is said to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. In some parts of the world, its leaves and stems are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is also believed to possess antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dalzellia ceylanica for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Dalzellia ceylanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Dalzellia, which is a part of the large and diverse family of daisies and sunflowers. The exact classification within the plant kingdom is: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids. This classification reflects the plant's relationship to other flowering plants and its evolutionary history. Further classification within the family Asteraceae is: Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Dalzellia.
benefits: Dalzellia ceylanica is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Sri Lanka. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. Its extracts may also possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, potentially aiding in the treatment of various infections and diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential uses. Further studies may uncover more significant advantages of using Dalzellia ceylanica in medical applications.
parts: Dalzellia ceylanica roots and leaves are reportedly used in traditional medicine. However, more research is needed to confirm their efficacy and potential health benefits.
